title: poa pratensis subsp. rigens
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. rigens, also known as tall meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the common meadow-grass plant (Poa pratensis) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Poa pratensis subsp. rigens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
